chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Criminal Miscellaneous Application No. S–745 of 2025. (Munwar Ali vs The State and others) Date of hearing Order with the signature of the Judge Fresh case 1. For orders on office objection at flag ‘A’ 2. For orders on MA No.6870/2025 (Ex.A) 3. For the hearing of the main case 09.03.2026. None Present. ************ On the last date of hearing, this Court had observed that after filing of the instant Criminal Miscellaneous Application, no effort had been made by the applicant to place the matter before the Court for hearing. The case was, however, adjourned with a note of caution that if on the next date none appeared on behalf of the applicant, the application would be decided based on the material available on record. Today, no one has appeared, and no intimation has been received. In pursuance thereof, the available record has been perused. It appears that the applicant/complainant had moved an application before the trial Court for joining the respondent No.03 and 04 as accused under Section 193 Cr.P.C. However, the said application was dismissed by the trial Court vide order dated 24.10.2025. A perusal of the impugned order reflects that no sufficient material was available on record to justify the joinder of the respondents as accused persons. It is a settled principle of law that an accused can only be summoned when sufficient incriminating material is available on record. In the present case, the complainant/applicant failed to point out any such material before the trial Court. In these circumstances, no ground is made out to interfere with the impugned order.

In the present case, the complainant/applicant failed to point out any such material before the trial Court. In these circumstances, no ground is made out to interfere with the impugned order. Consequently, the instant Criminal Miscellaneous Application, being devoid of merit, is hereby dismissed, along with the listed application. The office is directed to communicate this order to the learned trial Court. JUDGE ARBROHI
